orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.content:
**Orcas Island Forecast – Friday, Feb 20**

☁️ Mostly cloudy, high 37°F. Wind chill 19-29°F – dress warmly!

**Ferry Travel:** Favorable conditions during the day with calm SE winds (~9 mph). Evening brings stronger gusts to 21 mph – plan crossings earlier if possible. Check WSF for updates.

**Outdoor Activities:** Dry daytime window ideal for hiking or exploring. Bundle up for cold wind chills. Avoid evening outings – 20% chance of snow showers after 10pm.

**Looking Ahead:** Saturday turns unsettled with 40% snow/rain chance and stronger winds. Best outdoor day is tomorrow morning.
orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.rawContent:
## Tomorrow's Weather Forecast for Orcas Island, WA (Friday, Feb 20)

### Conditions
- **Sky:** Mostly cloudy
- **High:** 37°F
- **Low:** 35°F (Friday night)
- **Wind Chill:** 19-29°F

### Precipitation
- **Daytime:** Dry
- **Evening:** 20% chance of snow showers after 10pm

### Wind Conditions
- **Daytime:** Southeast wind around 9 mph (calm for ferry travel)
- **Evening:** East wind 13-16 mph, gusts up to 21 mph

### Travel Advisory
Tomorrow looks favorable for island travel with light winds during the day. Evening conditions worsen with increased winds and possible snow showers. Ferry service should operate normally, though travelers should check with Washington State Ferries for any weather-related updates.

**Note:** Marine forecast shows conditions improving from today's blustery NE winds (18-25 mph, gusts to 32). Saturday brings more unsettled weather with 40% chance of snow/rain and stronger winds (gusts to 24 mph).

**Morning:** Mostly cloudy, calm winds. Low near 36°F.

### Conditions Throughout the Day

Overall a cool, mostly cloudy day with dry conditions expected during daylight hours, but increasing chance of precipitation in the evening.

wa-road-conditions.content:
# Washington State Road Conditions Summary
**February 19, 2026**

## I-5 (Seattle Corridor)
🟡 **DELAYS** - Ship Canal Bridge reduced to 2 lanes; express lanes northbound-only. NE 145th St interchange has 24/7 lane closures.

## I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass)
✅ **CLEAR** - Pass open, no restrictions. Road bare/wet with some snow patches. 27°F.

⚠️ **CAUTION** - Exit 174 (Moses Lake) overpass closed; use Exit 169 detour.

## Highway 20 (North Cascades)
🔴 **CLOSED** - Winter closure MP 134-171. Reopens late spring.

## SR-20 Eastern Passes
✅ **CLEAR** - Loup Loup, Wauconda, Sherman passes open with compact snow/ice. No restrictions.

## SR-20 to Anacortes
✅ **CLEAR** - Normal operations to ferry terminal.

---
**Reminder:** Traction tires advised on Stevens, Mt. Baker, Crystal-Greenwater, and White passes.
wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
I now have comprehensive data. Let me compile the road conditions report:

---

# 🚗 Washington State Road Conditions Report
**As of: February 19, 2026, 6:33 AM PST**

---

## I-5 (Seattle Corridor)

### ⚠️ HIGH IMPACT ALERTS

| Location | Impact | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **Ship Canal Bridge** | 🔴 HIGH | I-5 mainline reduced to **2 lanes** due to northbound bridge preservation work. Express lanes operating **northbound-only** to maintain capacity. |
| **NE 130th - Northgate Way** | 🟡 MEDIUM | Tonight (8:30pm-4am): 2 left lanes SB and 1 left lane NB closed for maintenance |
| **SR 523/NE 145th St** | 🔴 HIGH | Lane shifts and 24/7 closures on 5th, 6th, 8th Ave NE near I-5 interchange |
| **Stewart/Denny - Mercer St** | 🟢 LOW | Friday night (10pm-6am): 1 left NB lane closed for maintenance |

---

## I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass)

### ✅ PASS OPEN - No Restrictions

| Condition | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| **Temperature** | 27°F |
| **Road Surface** | Bare and wet, snow and slush in places |
| **Weather** | Overcast skies |
| **Eastbound** | No restrictions |
| **Westbound** | No restrictions |

### ⚠️ Other I-90 Alerts

| Location | Impact | Details |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **Exit 174/Hansen Rd (Moses Lake)** | 🔴 HIGH | Overpass **CLOSED** due to bridge deck/pier deterioration. No ETA for reopening. EB can still exit, WB must use Exit 169 detour. |
| **Ritzville to SR 904** | 🟢 LOW | Bare and dry, wet in places |
| **US 195 to Broadway (Spokane)** | 🟢 LOW | Bare and dry |

---

## Highway 20 (North Cascades)

### 🔴 PASS CLOSED

| Condition | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| **Status** | **CLOSED for winter** (since Dec 4, 2025) |
| **Closure** | Milepost 134 to 171 (Ross Dam Trailhead to Silver Star Gate) |
| **Reopening** | Typically late spring (late April-May) depending on conditions |

### Other SR 20 Passes

| Pass | Status | Conditions |
|------|--------|------------|
| **Loup Loup Pass** | ✅ OPEN | 17°F, compact snow/ice, light snow falling, no restrictions |
| **Wauconda Pass** | ✅ OPEN | 14°F, compact snow/ice, occasional snow flurries |
| **Sherman Pass** | ✅ OPEN | 15°F, compact snow/ice, light snow, no restrictions |

---

## SR-20 to Anacortes (San Juan Islands Ferry)

### ✅ ROUTE OPEN - Normal Operations

No active alerts affecting SR-20 from Burlington to Anacortes ferry terminal.

### 🚢 Anacortes Ferry Terminal

| Status | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Terminal** | Open with all amenities (waiting room, food service, restrooms) |
| **San Juan Islands Routes** | Operating normally |
| **Upcoming Change** | 3% card payment fee starting March 1, 2026 |

---

## ❄️ Winter Driving Reminders

- **Traction tires advised** on Stevens Pass (US 2), Mt. Baker Hwy (SR 542), Crystal-Greenwater (SR 410), White Pass (US 12)
- **Carry chains** - conditions can change rapidly
- **Check conditions before departing**: wsdot.com/travel

---

**Sources**: WSDOT Highway Alerts API, Mountain Pass Conditions API, WSF Alerts

travel-summary.content:
# TRAVEL ADVISORY: Seattle → Orcas Island
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

## Overall Recommendation: GOOD TRAVEL DAY ✅

Tomorrow morning offers favorable conditions for your trip. Depart Seattle by 8:00–9:00 AM to maximize the dry, calm window.

---

**Route Conditions:**
- SR-20 to Anacortes: Clear, normal operations
- I-5 Seattle: Expect 20-30 min delays at Ship Canal Bridge—leave early
- Ferry crossing: Calm winds (~9 mph), ideal conditions through midday

**Weather Summary:**
- Seattle: Dry, cloudy, 36-43°F
- Orcas Island: Dry until evening, high 37°F, wind chill 19-29°F
- Evening deterioration: Winds gusting to 21 mph, 20-30% rain/snow chance after 10pm

**Key Alerts:**
- ⚠️ Return ferry before 5pm to avoid rougher evening crossings
- ⚠️ Extreme wind chill on Orcas—limit outdoor exposure
- ⚠️ Saturday weather worsens (40% precip, stronger winds)

**Pack List:**
- Warm layers, hat, gloves, windproof jacket
- Umbrella for evening
- Full gas tank, snacks/water
- WSF app for ferry status

**Bottom Line:** Go tomorrow. Morning conditions are ideal, but weather deteriorates by evening. Early departure, midday island time, and an afternoon return ferry give you the best experience. Saturday will be worse—this is your window.
travel-summary.rawContent:
# 🚗⛴️ Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

## 1. Current Conditions (Feb 19)

| Location | Temp | Conditions | Concerns |
|----------|------|------------|----------|
| Seattle | 36°F (feels 33°F) | Cloudy, 93% humidity | Slight rain/snow chance until 1pm |
| SR-20 to Anacortes | ✅ Clear | Normal operations | None |
| I-5 Seattle | 🟡 Delays | Lane reductions | Ship Canal Bridge, NE 145th |

---

## 2. Tomorrow's Travel Outlook

**Morning–Afternoon: ✅ FAVORABLE**
- Seattle: Dry, cloudy, 36-43°F, calm winds
- Ferry route: Calm SE winds ~9 mph, ideal crossing conditions
- Orcas Island: Dry window, high 37°F (wind chill 19-29°F)

**Evening: ⚠️ DETERIORATING**
- Seattle: 30% rain chance after 10pm
- Ferry: Winds gusting to 21 mph – rougher crossings
- Orcas: 20% snow showers after 10pm

---

## 3. Recommended Departure Time

**🕗 Leave Seattle by 8:00–9:00 AM**

| Reason | Details |
|--------|---------|
| I-5 delays | Allow extra 20-30 min for Ship Canal Bridge congestion |
| Ferry timing | Catch mid-morning sailing for calmest conditions |
| Daylight | Maximize dry daytime window on Orcas |
| Return buffer | Complete activities before evening wind pickup |

---

## 4. What to Pack/Prepare

- 🧥 **Warm layers** – Wind chill to 19°F on Orcas
- 🧤 **Hat, gloves, windproof jacket**
- ☂️ **Umbrella** – For evening rain possibility
- 📱 **WSF app** – Check ferry status: [wsdot.com/ferries](https://wsdot.com/ferries)
- ⛽ **Full tank** – Limited services on island
- 🍫 **Snacks/water** – In case of ferry delays

---

## 5. ⚠️ Alerts & Concerns

| Priority | Issue | Action |
|----------|-------|--------|
| 🟡 Medium | I-5 Ship Canal Bridge delays | Depart early, consider alternate routes |
| 🟡 Medium | Evening wind gusts (21 mph) | **Return ferry before 5pm** if possible |
| 🟡 Medium | Extreme wind chill on Orcas | Limit outdoor exposure, dress in layers |
| 🟢 Low | Evening rain/snow chance | Minor; carry umbrella |
| 📅 FYI | Saturday weather worsens | 40% precip, stronger winds – tomorrow is better |

---

**Bottom Line:** Tomorrow morning is your ideal travel window. Depart Seattle early to avoid I-5 delays, take a mid-morning ferry, enjoy the dry daytime on Orcas, and aim to return before evening winds intensify. Saturday looks worse—go tomorrow!
